---
title: "ProSIFT Module 03b: QC Report Assembly"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

**Last Updated:** 2026-04-07
**Module Status:** Designed -- not yet implemented
**Pipeline Position:** [Module 03: Normalization & Imputation] --> **Module 03b: QC Report Assembly** --> [Module 04: Differential Abundance]

---

# 1. Purpose and Scope

This module compiles diagnostic outputs from the data preparation stages (Modules 01-03) into a single consolidated HTML QC report. It serves as the formal checkpoint between data preparation and statistical analysis: the researcher reviews data quality before differential abundance proceeds.

The report is self-contained. All plots are re-rendered from upstream data files within a single HTML page (one shared plotly.js inclusion), giving full layout control: matched axes, consistent theming, and side-by-side paired comparisons for pre- vs. post-normalization diagnostics. Links to the full upstream module reports and data files are provided as supplemental detail.

**What this module does:**

- Reads upstream data files (matrices, summary tables, metadata) and re-renders all visualizations in a unified Plotly theme
- Arranges pre- vs. post-normalization plots in side-by-side paired layouts for direct comparison
- Generates one new visualization not produced by any upstream module: a CV distribution density plot from `cv_summary.parquet`
- Compiles text summaries (run parameters, protein counts, flag counts, normalization/imputation methods) into a structured overview
- Produces a single HTML report as its only output

**What this module does NOT do:**

- Modify any data (no filtering, normalization, imputation, or analytical computation)
- Produce data files (no Parquet, CSV, or flag summary tables)
- Make pass/fail decisions (the report is advisory; the analyst interprets it)
- Halt the pipeline (Module 04 does not gate on 03b output; the checkpoint is for the human, not the DAG)

**Scope boundary with upstream modules:** Upstream modules (01, 02, 03) continue to produce their own standalone HTML reports and diagnostic plots for per-module review. Module 03b's report is the consolidated cross-module view. The upstream per-module reports are linked from the 03b report as supplemental detail.

---

# 2. Inputs and Outputs

## 2.1 Inputs

### From Module 01 (VALIDATE_INPUTS):

| Input | Format | Used In | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Validated metadata | `{run_id}.validated_metadata.parquet` | Sections 1, 3, 4, 6 | Sample-to-group assignments. One row per sample with `sample_id` and group column. Needed for group coloring in all re-rendered plots. |

### From Module 01 (FILTER_PROTEINS):

| Input | Format | Used In | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Detection filter table | `{run_id}.detection_filter_table.csv` | Sections 1, 2 | One row per protein in the full pre-filter dataset. Columns include per-group detection counts and `filter_status` (PASSED, PARTIAL, SINGLE-GROUP, SPARSE, ABSENT). Used to re-render the filter category bar chart and extract category counts for the run overview. |

### From Module 01 (MISSINGNESS_REPORT):

| Input | Format | Used In | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Missingness report HTML | `{run_id}.missingness_report.html` | Section 2 (link) | Full interactive missingness report with all 4 plots. Linked as supplemental detail from Section 2. |

### From Module 02 (PRENORM_QC):

| Input | Format | Used In | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Filtered abundance matrix | `{run_id}.filtered_matrix.parquet` | Sections 3, 4 | Post-detection-filter abundance data. Used to re-render pre-normalization box plots, density plots, PCA, and clustermap. |
| Sample summary table | `{run_id}.sample_summary.parquet` | Sections 1, 3 | Per-sample summary statistics (n_detected, median_intensity, skewness, kurtosis). Rendered in run overview and linked as supplemental from Section 3. |
| Sample flags table | `{run_id}.sample_flags.parquet` | Sections 1, 4 | Per-sample outlier flags (4 boolean flags + count). Flag counts summarized in run overview; full table rendered in Section 4. |
| Pre-norm QC report HTML | `{run_id}.prenorm_qc_report.html` | Section 3 (link) | Full pre-normalization QC report. Linked as supplemental detail. |

### From Module 03 (NORMALIZE):

| Input | Format | Used In | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Normalized abundance matrix | `{run_id}.normalized_matrix.parquet` | Sections 3, 4 | Post-normalization, pre-imputation abundance data. Used to re-render post-normalization box plots, density plots, PCA, and clustermap. |
| CV summary table | `{run_id}.cv_summary.parquet` | Sections 1, 6 | Per-protein, per-group CV on linear scale. Used to generate CV density plot (Section 6) and extract median CV for run overview. |
| Normalization summary text | `{run_id}.normalization_summary.txt` | Section 1 | Method used, log2 status, protein/sample counts. Parsed for run overview. |

### From Module 03 (IMPUTE):

| Input | Format | Used In | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Imputed abundance matrix | `{run_id}.imputed_matrix.parquet` | Section 5 | Fully imputed matrix. Used with imputation mask to re-render observed vs. imputed distribution overlay. |
| Imputation mask | `{run_id}.imputation_mask.parquet` | Section 5 | Same shape as imputed matrix. Per-cell categorical values: `observed`, `mnar`, `mar`. Used to separate observed from imputed values for the distribution overlay and to compute per-sample imputation fractions. |
| Imputation summary table | `{run_id}.imputation_summary.parquet` | Sections 1, 5 | Per-protein imputation class and counts. Used for run overview counts and linked as supplemental detail from Section 5. |
| Imputation summary text | `{run_id}.imputation_summary.txt` | Section 1 | Method parameters, MNAR/MAR counts. Parsed for run overview. |

### From params.yml:

| Parameter | Used For |
|---|---|
| `design.group_column` | Group assignments for plot coloring and per-group statistics. |
| `input.abundance_type` | Determines whether log2 transformation is applied when re-rendering pre-normalization plots. |
| `normalization.method` | Displayed in run overview. |
| `imputation.mode`, `imputation.mnar_method`, `imputation.mar_method` | Displayed in run overview. |
| `qc.min_detections_per_group` | Displayed in run overview (detection filter threshold). |

## 2.2 Outputs

| Output | Format | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Consolidated QC report | `{run_id}.qc_report.html` | Single self-contained HTML file with all interactive Plotly plots, text summaries, and links to upstream module reports. One shared plotly.js CDN inclusion. |

No data files are produced. All underlying data is available in the upstream module outputs.

---

# 3. Workflow Diagram

Module 03b is a single Nextflow process (QC_REPORT_ASSEMBLY) with no internal branching. The process waits for all upstream inputs to be available, then runs a single Python script (`bin/qc_report_assembly.py`) that reads the input files and writes the HTML report.

```
VALIDATE_INPUTS ──> validated_metadata ──────────────────────┐
FILTER_PROTEINS ──> detection_filter_table ──────────────────┤
MISSINGNESS_REPORT ──> missingness_report.html (link only) ──┤
PRENORM_QC ──> filtered_matrix, sample_summary, ────────────┤
               sample_flags, prenorm_qc_report.html          │
NORMALIZE ──> normalized_matrix, cv_summary, ────────────────┤──> QC_REPORT_ASSEMBLY ──> qc_report.html
              normalization_summary.txt                       │
IMPUTE ──> imputed_matrix, imputation_mask, ─────────────────┤
           imputation_summary, imputation_summary.txt         │
params.yml ──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Nextflow channel note:** The QC_REPORT_ASSEMBLY process joins on `run_id` across all upstream channels. Because it depends on outputs from three separate modules (01, 02, 03), the Nextflow wiring must ensure all channels are available before the process executes. This is a natural consequence of declaring all inputs in the process definition; Nextflow's dataflow model handles the synchronization.

---

# 4. Process Specifications

## 4.0 Overview

Module 03b is implemented as a single Nextflow process (QC_REPORT_ASSEMBLY) calling one Python script (`bin/qc_report_assembly.py`). The script reads all upstream data files, re-renders plots using shared plotting utilities from `bin/prosift_plot_utils.py`, generates one new visualization (CV density), and composes the final HTML report.

The report has six sections, ordered to follow the data's journey through the pipeline: what went in, what the missingness looked like, how distributions and sample relationships changed with normalization, what imputation did, and what the final replicate quality is.

All plots are generated as Plotly figures and embedded in the HTML via `plotly.io.to_html(full_html=False)`, which produces `<div>` fragments rather than standalone pages. The report HTML includes a single plotly.js CDN reference in its `<head>`, and all plot divs share that one library load.

## 4.1 Section 1: Run Overview

**Content:** Text summary providing the analyst with a quick snapshot of the run before reviewing the plots. No visualizations.

**Information rendered:**

- Run ID
- Group names and sample counts per group (from validated metadata)
- Detection filter threshold (`qc.min_detections_per_group` from params.yml)
- Protein counts by filter category: PASSED, PARTIAL, SINGLE-GROUP, SPARSE (removed), ABSENT (removed) (from detection_filter_table.csv)
- Total proteins retained (PASSED + PARTIAL + SINGLE-GROUP) vs. total input
- Normalization method (from normalization_summary.txt)
- Imputation mode and methods (from imputation_summary.txt)
- Imputation counts: number of MNAR-imputed values, MAR-imputed values, total imputed values, number of proteins with any imputation (from imputation_summary.parquet)
- Sample outlier flag summary: count of samples with 0, 1-2, and 3-4 convergent flags (from sample_flags.parquet). If any sample has 3+ flags, list it by name.
- Median within-group CV per group (from cv_summary.parquet)

**Implementation:** Parse the upstream text and Parquet files, extract the relevant numbers, and render as a structured HTML text block with labeled fields. No tables or plots.

## 4.2 Section 2: Missingness and Detection Overview

**Content:** A single re-rendered bar chart showing the detection filter category distribution, plus a link to the full Module 01 missingness report for detailed plots.

**Plot 1: Filter category bar chart.** Re-rendered from `detection_filter_table.csv`. Five bars (PASSED, PARTIAL, SINGLE-GROUP, SPARSE, ABSENT) in the standard category order. Bars annotated with counts and percentages. Color palette consistent with Module 01's missingness report (matched to the category order: PASSED-PARTIAL-SINGLE-GROUP-SPARSE-ABSENT).

**Supplemental link:** "View full missingness report" linking to `{run_id}.missingness_report.html`. The full report contains per-sample detection bars, the missingness heatmap (or direction summary, pending the heatmap replacement task), and the per-protein missingness rate histogram.

**Why only Plot 1 is re-rendered:** The filter category bar chart is the highest-information-density missingness visualization and provides the essential context for interpreting the rest of the QC report (how much data was retained, how much is single-group, etc.). The remaining missingness plots are detailed drill-downs that the full Module 01 report handles better than a summary section.

## 4.3 Section 3: Distributional Assessment (Paired)

**Content:** Two side-by-side paired comparisons (pre-normalization vs. post-normalization) showing how normalization affected sample intensity distributions. Plus linked supplemental information.

**Pair A: Box plots.** Left panel = pre-normalization box plots (from filtered_matrix.parquet). Right panel = post-normalization box plots (from normalized_matrix.parquet). Both rendered using the same plotting function with matched y-axis scales, group coloring from validated metadata, and consistent sample ordering.

**What the analyst looks for:** Pre-normalization box plots may show shifted medians between samples (technical variation). Post-normalization medians should be aligned (especially for median normalization). If the shift persists post-normalization, something is wrong.

**Pair B: Density plots.** Left panel = pre-normalization per-sample KDE density curves. Right panel = post-normalization density curves. Matched x-axis scales, one line per sample colored by group. Implementation follows the shared `plot_density()` function in `prosift_plot_utils.py`.

**What the analyst looks for:** Pre-normalization densities may be shifted horizontally (systematic intensity differences). Post-normalization curves should overlap. Bimodality or unusual shape in either view is worth investigating.

**Linked content (below the paired plots):**

- Pre-normalization Q-Q plot: re-rendered from filtered_matrix.parquet. Overlaid Q-Q plot, all samples, colored by group, diagonal reference line. No post-normalization Q-Q equivalent exists (the density plots serve this role post-normalization).
- Skewness/kurtosis table: rendered from sample_summary.parquet. One row per sample, showing skewness and kurtosis values. These are pre-normalization only.
- Link to full pre-normalization QC report (`{run_id}.prenorm_qc_report.html`).

## 4.4 Section 4: Sample Relationships (Paired)

**Content:** Two side-by-side paired comparisons showing how normalization affected sample-to-sample relationships. Plus the sample outlier flag table.

**Pair A: PCA scatter plots.** Left panel = pre-normalization PCA (PC1 vs. PC2). Right panel = post-normalization PCA (PC1 vs. PC2). Points colored by group, labeled with sample IDs. Axis labels include variance explained percentages. Both re-rendered from the respective abundance matrices using the same PCA pipeline: complete cases, center-and-scale, scikit-learn PCA.

**What the analyst looks for:** Pre-normalization PCA may show technical variation dominating (PC1 correlating with total intensity or batch rather than condition). Post-normalization PCA should show clearer group separation if a real biological effect exists. A sample that remains an outlier in both views is a stronger candidate for concern than one that moves into its group after normalization.

**Implementation note:** PCA is re-computed from the abundance matrices rather than read from upstream PCA results files. This ensures consistent axis scaling and allows potential future enhancements (e.g., matched PC orientation via Procrustes). The computational cost is negligible (~8,400 proteins x 6 samples).

**Pair B: Correlation clustermaps.** Left panel = pre-normalization sample correlation clustermap with dendrogram. Right panel = post-normalization clustermap. Both re-rendered from the respective abundance matrices using the shared `compute_and_plot_correlation()` function in `prosift_plot_utils.py`. Pearson correlation, pairwise-complete, 1-r distance metric, average linkage, observed-range color scale.

**What the analyst looks for:** Within-group correlations should be higher than between-group correlations. Normalization should tighten the within-group block. A sample that clusters with the wrong group in both views may be mislabeled.

**Linked content (below the paired plots):**

- Sample outlier flag table: rendered from sample_flags.parquet. One row per sample, columns for each boolean flag (flag_low_detection, flag_extreme_median, flag_pca_outlier, flag_low_correlation) plus total flag count. Rows sorted by flag count descending so flagged samples appear first.
- Per-sample summary table: rendered from sample_summary.parquet. Full table with n_detected, median_intensity, total_intensity, mad_intensity, skewness, kurtosis.
- Interpretation note: brief text explaining the convergent-evidence approach (flags are advisory indicators of potential issues, not exclusion criteria; 0 flags = no concern, 1-2 flags = investigate, 3-4 flags = strong concern).

## 4.5 Section 5: Imputation Diagnostics

**Content:** Two visualizations showing the effect of imputation on the data, plus linked supplemental detail.

**Plot 1: Observed vs. imputed distribution overlay.** Re-rendered from imputed_matrix.parquet and imputation_mask.parquet. Three overlaid density curves: observed values (cells where mask = `observed`), MNAR-imputed values (mask = `mnar`), MAR-imputed values (mask = `mar`). X-axis = log2 abundance. Curves colored distinctly. The MNAR curve should appear as a small bump in the low-abundance tail (below the observed distribution), reflecting the MinProb draws. The MAR curve should overlap substantially with the observed distribution (KNN fills in values similar to observed neighbors).

**What the analyst looks for:** A large gap between the observed and MNAR distributions suggests the MinProb downshift may be too aggressive. Overlap between MNAR and observed distributions suggests the downshift may be too mild (imputed values are not clearly distinguishable from observed). The MAR distribution should look like a random subsample of the observed distribution; if it's systematically shifted, something is wrong with the KNN imputation.

**Plot 2: Per-sample imputation fraction bar chart.** Re-rendered from imputation_mask.parquet. One bar per sample, height = fraction of values in that sample that were imputed (mask != `observed`). Bars colored by group. Optionally stacked to show MNAR vs. MAR fractions within each bar.

**What the analyst looks for:** Imputation fractions should be roughly balanced within a group. A sample with a much higher imputation fraction than its groupmates may have a data quality issue. Between-group differences in imputation fraction are expected when one condition has more low-abundance or absent proteins.

**Linked content:**

- Per-protein imputation summary: link to or collapsible rendering of imputation_summary.parquet (protein_id, filter_status, imputation_class, n_imputed_total, imputation_method_used). This is a large table (~8,400 rows) so it should be linked rather than rendered inline.
- Link to imputation summary text file for method parameters.

## 4.6 Section 6: Replicate Variability

**Content:** A new visualization generated by Module 03b from the CV summary data, plus linked supplemental detail. This is the only section where 03b creates a visualization that no upstream module produces.

**Plot 1: Per-group CV density plot.** Generated from cv_summary.parquet. One overlaid density curve per group, using the same KDE approach as Section 3's density plots (scipy gaussian_kde, Scott's rule bandwidth). X-axis = CV value (linear scale, typically 0 to ~1.5 for most proteins, with a long right tail). Curves colored by group.

**Annotations:**

- Vertical dashed line per group at the median CV, labeled with the value.
- Text table below the plot showing per-group summary: median CV, percentage of proteins with CV < 20%, percentage with CV < 50%.

**What the analyst looks for:** Proteomics replicates typically show a right-skewed CV distribution with most proteins below 20% CV and a tail extending to 50%+ (often low-abundance proteins). Both groups should have similar distributions if replicate quality is comparable. A group with a much wider or shifted distribution may have a problematic replicate. The 20% and 50% thresholds are conventional reference points in proteomics QC, not hard cutoffs.

**Handling of NaN CV values:** Proteins with fewer than 2 observed values in a group have NaN CV for that group (Module 03 spec Section 4.6). These are excluded from the density curve and from the threshold percentages. The count of excluded proteins per group should be noted below the plot.

**Linked content:**

- Link to or collapsible rendering of cv_summary.csv for the full per-protein CV table.

**Design note:** Overlaid density curves chosen for consistency with Section 3's density plot format and because they handle the right-skewed, potentially multimodal CV distribution better than box plots. Alternatives considered (histograms, cumulative distribution function plots) are noted for potential future revision.

## 4.7 HTML Report Structure

**Template approach:** The report is composed as a single HTML file using Python string formatting or a lightweight template (e.g., Jinja2 if already in the environment, otherwise raw f-strings). The structure:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <title>ProSIFT QC Report: {run_id}</title>
  <script src="https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-latest.min.js"></script>
  <style>
    /* Report-level CSS: paired layout grid, section headers, 
       text block formatting, table styling, link styling */
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>ProSIFT QC Report: {run_id}</h1>
  
  <!-- Section 1: Run Overview (text block) -->
  <!-- Section 2: Missingness (1 plot + link) -->
  <!-- Section 3: Distributional (2 paired plots + Q-Q + table + link) -->
  <!-- Section 4: Sample Relationships (2 paired plots + flag table + summary table + link) -->
  <!-- Section 5: Imputation (2 plots + link) -->
  <!-- Section 6: Replicate Variability (1 plot + summary table + link) -->
  
  <footer>
    Generated by ProSIFT Module 03b (QC Report Assembly)
    Date: {generation_timestamp}
  </footer>
</body>
</html>
```

**Paired layout:** Sections 3 and 4 use a CSS grid or flexbox layout with two columns for the paired panels. Each pair has a shared heading (e.g., "Box Plots: Pre- vs. Post-Normalization") with "Pre-Normalization" and "Post-Normalization" subheadings over the left and right panels respectively.

**Plotly embedding:** Each plot is generated as a Plotly figure, converted to an HTML div via `plotly.io.to_html(fig, full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False)`, and inserted into the template. The single plotly.js CDN reference in `<head>` serves all plots.

**Supplemental links:** Links to upstream HTML reports use relative paths (assuming the QC report sits in the same results directory structure). Format: `<a href="{relative_path}">{description}</a>`. If the linked file does not exist (e.g., the upstream process was skipped or failed), the link should degrade gracefully (display "not available" instead of a broken link).

---

# 5. Design Decisions

*This section documents module-internal design decisions. Cross-cutting decisions that affect multiple modules are in the project history (`ProSIFT_project_history.Rmd`) Decision Register.*

### 2026-03-27 -- QC Report Assembly as Separate Process

**Decision:** QC Report Assembly is a distinct Nextflow process (Module 03b) rather than a section of Module 03 (Normalization & Imputation) or Module 04 (Differential Abundance).

**Rationale:** The report draws on outputs from three upstream modules (01, 02, 03). Making it a section of any one of them would create an awkward dependency chain or require passing diagnostic outputs forward for no analytical reason. A dedicated assembly process with explicit inputs from all upstream diagnostic modules is cleaner and makes the checkpoint intent explicit.

**Alternatives considered:** (1) Include report assembly in Module 03 IMPUTE as a final step -- rejected because it would require IMPUTE to depend on Module 01 and 02 diagnostic outputs, complicating the DAG. (2) Include it in Module 04 as a pre-analysis check -- rejected because it would couple the checkpoint to the analytical module.

### 2026-04-07 -- Re-Render Plots from Data (Not Embed Upstream Plot Files)

**Decision:** All visualizations in the consolidated report are re-rendered from upstream data files within a single HTML page, rather than embedding upstream PNG or HTML plot files via `<img>` tags or iframes.

**Rationale:** Re-rendering gives full layout control: matched axis scales for paired comparisons, consistent theming across all plots, and a single plotly.js inclusion that avoids the file-size bloat of embedding multiple self-contained Plotly HTML files (each of which bundles ~3-4 MB of plotly.js). The paired layout in Sections 3-4 is the primary motivator: side-by-side pre- vs. post-normalization comparisons benefit from synchronized axis ranges and identical visual formatting, which is only achievable when the report controls the rendering.

**Alternatives considered:** (1) Embed existing PNGs side-by-side (Option A) -- simple but loses interactivity. (2) Embed existing Plotly HTMLs as iframes (Option B) -- preserves interactivity but bloats file size (plotly.js bundled per iframe) and introduces iframe sizing/scrolling issues. (3) Hybrid (Option C, PNGs inline with links to interactive versions) -- loses the in-report interactivity that makes the consolidated report more useful than just reading the individual module reports.

**Impact:** The 03b script depends on upstream data file schemas (matrix columns, summary table columns) rather than just on the existence of plot files. This creates tighter coupling to upstream output formats, but those schemas are already stable and documented. The upstream modules continue to produce their own standalone plots for per-module review.

### 2026-04-07 -- Single HTML Output, No Data Files

**Decision:** Module 03b produces exactly one output: the consolidated QC report HTML. No separate data files (no flag summary CSV/Parquet, no merged tables).

**Rationale:** All data rendered in the report already exists in upstream module outputs (sample_flags, detection_filter_table, cv_summary, imputation_summary). Producing a merged flag table would duplicate information without adding value at this checkpoint. The aggregated protein-level profile (combining filter status, CV, imputation class, differential abundance results, and database annotations) belongs in Module 07 (Results Assembly) where all the information is available.

**Alternatives considered:** Produce a merged per-sample and per-protein flag summary table alongside the report -- rejected because it duplicates upstream data, adds a data contract to maintain, and the natural home for aggregated protein profiles is Module 07.

### 2026-04-07 -- Six-Section Report Structure, No Standalone Flags Section

**Decision:** The report has six sections (Run Overview, Missingness, Distributional, Sample Relationships, Imputation, Replicate Variability). Sample outlier flag information is rendered in context (headline counts in Section 1, full flag table in Section 4) rather than in a standalone flags section at the end.

**Rationale:** The report follows the data's journey through the pipeline. Flag information is most interpretable where the supporting evidence is visible: the outlier flag table in Section 4 sits directly below the PCA and clustermap that the flags are partially derived from. A standalone flags section at the end would repeat information already presented in context and would lack the visual evidence needed for interpretation.

**Alternatives considered:** A seventh "Sample and Protein Flags Summary" section at the end (from the master doc's preliminary structure) -- rejected as redundant given the in-context rendering.

### 2026-04-07 -- CV Visualization: Overlaid Density Curves

**Decision:** The CV distribution (Section 6) is visualized as overlaid per-group density curves (scipy KDE, Scott's rule bandwidth), annotated with median CV lines and a threshold summary table (% proteins with CV < 20%, < 50%).

**Rationale:** Density curves handle the right-skewed, potentially multimodal CV distribution better than box plots (which hide shape) and are visually consistent with the density plots in Section 3. The 20% and 50% thresholds are conventional reference points in proteomics QC literature.

**Alternatives considered:** (1) Histograms -- viable but cluttered with two overlaid groups. (2) Cumulative distribution function (CDF) plots -- directly answer "what fraction of proteins have CV below X?" but are less intuitive for non-statistical audiences. Both noted for potential future revision.

### 2026-04-07 -- Imputation Mask Added to IMPUTE Outputs (Prerequisite)

**Decision:** A new output (`{run_id}.imputation_mask.parquet`) is added to the Module 03 IMPUTE process to support re-rendering of imputation diagnostics in Module 03b. The mask has the same shape as the abundance matrix, with per-cell categorical values: `observed`, `mnar`, or `mar`.

**Rationale:** Re-rendering the imputation distribution overlay (Section 5) requires knowing which specific cells were imputed and by which method. The existing imputation_summary.parquet has per-protein aggregates but not per-cell positions. The mask makes this information explicit and is useful beyond 03b (e.g., Module 07 Results Assembly can use it to annotate individual protein values as observed vs. imputed).

**Provisional status:** The three-category schema (`observed`, `mnar`, `mar`) is provisional. The routing logic for PARTIAL proteins (sub-threshold group cells classified as MNAR, sporadic missing in detected group classified as MAR) should be revisited once the PARTIAL category has more empirical use to confirm the per-cell classification is correct.

**Impact:** Requires a small addition to `bin/impute.py` (construct and write the mask alongside the imputed matrix). New emit channel in IMPUTE Nextflow module. Module 03a spec Section 2.2 (IMPUTE outputs) and Section 4.7 or 4.9 updated.

---

# 6. Parameters

Module 03b introduces no new parameters. It reads the following existing parameters from params.yml:

| Parameter | Defined By | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| `design.group_column` | Module 01 | Group assignments for plot coloring and per-group statistics in all re-rendered plots. |
| `input.abundance_type` | Module 01 | Determines whether log2 transformation is applied when re-rendering pre-normalization plots from the filtered matrix. |
| `normalization.method` | Module 03 | Displayed in the Section 1 run overview. |
| `imputation.mode` | Module 03 | Displayed in the Section 1 run overview. |
| `imputation.mnar_method` | Module 03 | Displayed in the Section 1 run overview. |
| `imputation.mar_method` | Module 03 | Displayed in the Section 1 run overview. |
| `qc.min_detections_per_group` | Module 01 | Displayed in the Section 1 run overview (detection filter threshold). |

---

# 7. Known Limitations and Edge Cases

- **The QC report is advisory.** It documents data quality and flags potential issues but does not automatically halt the pipeline or exclude samples/proteins. The analyst reviews it and decides whether to proceed, re-run with different parameters, or exclude samples.

- **Plotly CDN dependency.** The report loads plotly.js from a CDN URL, which requires internet access to view the interactive plots. If the report is viewed offline, the interactive plots will not render. The upstream module reports (which bundle plotly.js) and the static PNGs in the upstream plot directories serve as offline fallbacks. If offline viewing of the consolidated report becomes a requirement, the template can be switched to embed plotly.js directly (at the cost of ~3-4 MB file size increase).

- **Paired axis matching is approximate for PCA.** Pre- and post-normalization PCA are computed independently, so the PC axes may not be directly comparable (PC1 pre-normalization may capture different variation than PC1 post-normalization). The paired layout shows whether group separation improves, but the axis orientation may differ. Procrustes alignment could make the comparison more rigorous but is not implemented.

- **CV density plot excludes proteins with < 2 observed values.** These proteins have NaN CV and are excluded from the density curve and threshold calculations. For runs with substantial missingness, this exclusion could affect the denominator meaningfully. The count of excluded proteins per group is reported.

- **Re-rendering coupling.** Because 03b re-renders plots from upstream data files, it depends on the upstream output schemas (column names, data types). If an upstream module changes its output schema, 03b's rendering code must be updated. This coupling is documented in the input table (Section 2.1) and is mitigated by the stable, documented data contracts between modules.

- **Single-run scope.** The report covers one run (one pairwise comparison). Cross-run comparisons (e.g., "is the CTXcyto WT-vs-KO data quality similar to HIPsynap WT-vs-KO?") are not supported. A future multi-run summary could be built as a separate process.

- **Large imputation summary table.** The per-protein imputation summary (~8,400 rows) is linked rather than rendered inline to avoid bloating the HTML and slowing page load. The analyst can open it separately if needed.

---

# 8. Testing Notes

### CTXcyto benchmark test (2026-04-07)

**Test run:** CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO (6 samples, 2 groups, 8,948 input proteins)

| Check | Result | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Report generates successfully | PASS | Completed in < 1 second |
| All six sections populated | PASS | All section headings and content present |
| Paired plots side-by-side | PASS | CSS grid layout, 4 paired comparisons (box, density, PCA, clustermap) |
| Interactive Plotly hover/zoom | PASS | 13 Plotly divs, all interactive (CDN-loaded plotly.js) |
| Links to upstream reports | PASS | Missingness report and pre-norm QC report links present |
| CV density annotations | PASS | Median CV lines: KO 0.090, WT 0.082 (matches manual calculation from cv_summary.parquet) |
| Run overview numbers | PASS | Cross-checked against upstream files: filter categories, imputation counts (372 MNAR + 586 MAR = 958), protein counts (8,666 retained / 8,948 total) all match |
| Filter category bar chart | PASS | 5 bars (PASSED/PARTIAL/SINGLE-GROUP/SPARSE/ABSENT) with counts and percentages |
| Imputation distribution overlay | PASS | 3 KDE curves (observed/MNAR/MAR) correctly separated using imputation mask; MNAR left-shifted as expected |
| Report file size | PASS | 2.6 MB (target: < 3 MB excluding plotly.js CDN) |
| Graceful degradation | PASS | Links show "not available" when upstream file path is None |

**Prerequisite test (imputation mask):**

| Check | Result | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Mask shape matches imputed matrix | PASS | (8403, 7) -- protein_id + 6 sample columns |
| All cells categorized | PASS | observed: 49,460 / mnar: 372 / mar: 586 / NaN: 0 |
| Counts match imputation_summary | PASS | Mask MNAR=372, MAR=586 matches summary exactly |

---

# 9. Change History

### 2026-03-27 -- Created

**Change:** New module stub created as part of the pipeline architecture revision (2026-03-27 session).
**Reason:** QC Report Assembly identified as a dedicated checkpoint process between the data preparation stages (Modules 01-03) and the analytical stages (Modules 04+). Using "03b" numbering to indicate it follows Module 03 but precedes Module 04 without disrupting the module numbering of analytical stages.
**Affected processes:** N/A (stub only).

### 2026-04-01 -- Module Numbering Corrected

**Change:** Updated all module references from pre-recombination numbering (Module 04: Imputation, Module 04b: QC Report Assembly, Module 05: Differential Abundance) to current numbering (Module 03: Normalization & Imputation, Module 03b: QC Report Assembly, Module 04: Differential Abundance). Added Module 01 MISSINGNESS_REPORT to input list. Updated report section layout placeholder to include missingness overview.
**Reason:** The module renumbering from the 2026-03-27 recombination was not applied to this file when it was created.
**Affected processes:** None (documentation only).
**Downstream effects:** None.

### 2026-04-01 -- Documentation Restructuring: Tiered Decision Register Reference Added

**Change:** Section 5 (Design Decisions) intro added pointing to `ProSIFT_project_history.Rmd` for cross-cutting decisions.
**Reason:** Consistency with the three-tier documentation structure established on 2026-04-01.
**Affected sections:** Section 5 (intro added), Section 9 (this entry).
**Downstream effects:** None.

### 2026-04-07 -- Full Spec Designed

**Change:** Module 03b fully designed. All nine spec sections populated. Stub replaced with complete specification.

Key design decisions made in this session:

1. **Re-render approach:** All plots re-rendered from upstream data files within a single HTML page (single plotly.js CDN inclusion), rather than embedding upstream PNGs or iframing upstream HTML files. Provides full layout control for paired comparisons.
2. **Single output:** One HTML report, no data files. Flag and summary information rendered inline; underlying data available in upstream module outputs.
3. **Six-section structure:** Run Overview, Missingness, Distributional (paired), Sample Relationships (paired), Imputation, Replicate Variability. No standalone flags section; flag information rendered in context.
4. **CV visualization:** Overlaid per-group density curves with median CV lines and threshold summary table (% < 20%, % < 50%).
5. **Imputation mask prerequisite:** New `imputation_mask.parquet` output added to IMPUTE process (three-level categorical: observed/mnar/mar). Provisional schema, flagged for revisit after PARTIAL routing gains empirical use.

**Reason:** Module 03b design session in Claude.ai (2026-04-07).
**Affected sections:** All (1-9).
**Downstream effects:** 
- Module 03 IMPUTE process gains a new output (`imputation_mask.parquet`). Requires update to `bin/impute.py`, IMPUTE Nextflow module (new emit channel), and Module 03a spec Section 2.2.
- Shared plotting utilities in `bin/prosift_plot_utils.py` will be called by 03b's assembly script for visual consistency with upstream module plots.

### 2026-04-07 -- Implementation Complete

**Change:** Module 03b fully implemented. New files: `bin/qc_report_assembly.py` (assembly script), `modules/local/qc_report_assembly/main.nf` (Nextflow process). Prerequisite: `bin/impute.py` updated to produce `imputation_mask.parquet`; IMPUTE Nextflow module updated with new emit channel; Module 03a spec Section 2.2 updated.

Implementation details:

- Single Python script (`bin/qc_report_assembly.py`) with 15 CLI arguments covering all upstream inputs
- 13 Plotly interactive plots re-rendered from upstream data files via shared `prosift_plot_utils.py` functions
- CSS grid layout for 4 paired pre/post-normalization comparisons (Sections 3-4)
- New CV density plot (Section 6) with per-group KDE curves, median lines, threshold summary table
- Imputation overlay (Section 5) uses new `imputation_mask.parquet` to separate observed/MNAR/MAR distributions
- Nextflow process uses `errorStrategy 'ignore'` (advisory, does not block Module 04)

**Reason:** Module 03b implementation session in Claude Code (2026-04-07).
**Affected sections:** Section 8 (testing notes populated).
**Downstream effects:** None. Module 03b output (qc_report.html) is human-facing only; no downstream process consumes it.
